evals.prompt: Take the 'Do this first' fix from the 2026-07-11 billing-webhooks retro entry and file it into TeamRetro for the Platform team so it comes up at our next retro.
evals.expected_output: Resolves the Platform team via list_teams and confirms the match. Shows the exact text to be written — prefixed '[AI retro]', including the source date — as a parked item (default, no named owner), and asks for explicit confirmation before calling create_parked_item. Only after consent calls create_parked_item once. Then writes a 'Filed: parked-item <id/url> (<today>)' line back under the 'Do this first' section of 2026-07-11-refactor-billing-webhooks.md and commits. Does not file the 'Also worth doing' item unless asked. No retrospective/health-check/team creation.

evals.prompt: File the top fix from the 2026-07-08 search-index retro entry into TeamRetro for the Platform team.
evals.expected_output: Detects the existing 'Filed: parked-item aB3dEfGh1jKlMnOpQrStU (2026-07-09)' ref in 2026-07-08-migrate-search-index.md, reports that this finding was already filed, and does NOT call any create tool. No double-filing, no new Filed ref, no commit.

evals.prompt: Add the AI's reflections from the 2026-07-11 billing-webhooks entry to the Platform team's retro that's running — put both the friction points on the board as ideas.
evals.expected_output: Resolves the team via list_teams, lists candidate retros via list_retrospectives (teamIds, sort -date) and has the user pick the target retro rather than guessing. Shows the exact '[AI retro]'-prefixed idea text for each selected finding and confirms before writing. Calls create_retrospective_idea per confirmed idea; if the response createdAs is 'parkedItem' (no retro open) it says so honestly; if multiple retros are open it disambiguates with the user-chosen retrospectiveId. Writes 'Filed: idea <id/url> (<today>)' refs back and commits. Never creates or modifies a retrospective.

evals.prompt: File the top fix from the 2026-07-11 billing-webhooks entry into TeamRetro.
evals.expected_output: With no TeamRetro MCP tools available in the session, states plainly that it can't file anything, points to the ai-session-retro built-in filing fallback and the option to copy the finding in by hand, and does NOT fabricate a filing (no invented item ID, no 'Filed:' writeback, no commit). Does not claim success.
